title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class: A100 at 20% (132/326), H100 in severe shortage at 10% (37/187), B200 tight at 14% (95/183), and H200 at 21% (53/189). H100 spot spiked 42.9% day-over-day to $2.86/hr on razor-thin availability, while H200 volatility hit 121.7% annualized, signaling panic bidding for next-gen silicon.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class: A100 at 20% (132/326), H100 in severe shortage at 10% (37/187), B200 tight at 14% (95/183), and H200 at 21% (53/189). H100 spot spiked 42.9% day-over-day to $2.86/hr on razor-thin availability, while H200 volatility hit 121.7% annualized, signaling panic bidding for next-gen silicon.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B on H100 runs $10.45/M tokens, meaning medium-tier API sellers at $3/M are selling at a loss of roughly $7.45 per million tokens. DeepSeek and Qwen price aggressively below $2/M output on comparable models: both are burning cash. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at premium tier with 90%+ margins. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $5.00/hr with the lowest inference cost ($3.66/M) and 92% premium-tier margins is undervalued compute given its TCO advantage over H100, which at $8.36/M inference cost and severe 10% availability is overpriced relative to throughput. Trade: rotate H100 capacity commitments into B200 and A100 where margin math actually works.
bullets: 10% of H100 GPUs are available, the tightest supply of any class tracked.
14% B200 availability with 85.5% utilization, up 5.6 percentage points in 30 days.
$2.86 median H100 spot is up 42.9% in a single day.
$0.06 per million output tokens is the floor, set by Meta Llama 3.1 8B and Gemma 2 2b.
21.76 is the LLMflation index, meaning frontier inference costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$80 per million output tokens for o3 Pro makes it the most expensive model tracked across 52 endpoints.
